text:
(necro's thread) oh I've built one of these!
The colorful track on the right is a 30-day chunk of the track on the left. Since the user can directly manipulate the 'small handle', I preserved its relative offset whenever possible (i.e. unless you scroll off an edge). I believe we got there on destination precision, from the scientists' perspective.
If we had to do (say) 3000 days, like a genome browser, I'd try adding a third track. Also, switching over to written queries and the find-all metaphor.
